bugs.error_message: POST /api/computer-games/:id/move -> 500 Internal server error. Prisma: Unique constraint failed on the fields: (gameId, ply) at tx.move.create (computer-games.service.ts:252)
bugs.root_cause: When the side-to-moves clock has flagged, engine applyMove() (game-state.ts isTimeout early-return) returns the game as completed WITHOUT appending the attempted move. submitMove ignored this: it took state.moves[last] (the PREVIOUS move) and re-inserted it, duplicating its ply -> unique (gameId,ply) violation -> 500. Triggered whenever a computer game sat idle past its time control.
bugs.fix: In submitMove, after applyMove, guard: if state.moves.length === engineState.moves.length (no move appended = flag fall), persist game as completed with state.result/resultReason (timeout) and return WITHOUT creating a Move row. Verified: flagged game now returns 200 status=completed result=white_wins resultReason=timeout, no dup row; normal moves ply1-3 still 200.

bugs.error_message: Uncaught Error: Maximum update depth exceeded. ... at ThemeSync (src/app/providers.tsx)
bugs.root_cause: PRE-EXISTING. ThemeSync had two bidirectional effects (theme->store via update(); store->theme via setTheme()). When the persisted settings-store appTheme disagreed with next-themes theme on load (e.g. localStorage purechess-settings appTheme='dark' but next-themes theme='system'), the two effects ping-ponged forever and never converged -> React 'Maximum update depth exceeded'. ThemeSync is global in Providers so it crashed EVERY page including the untouched homepage. Browser-localStorage triggered (not caused by the game redesign); surfaces for any user whose stored appTheme differs from next-themes. Restarting the dev server did NOT fix it; clearing localStorage did (confirming the trigger).
bugs.fix: Replaced the two effects with ONE reconciliation effect + a lastSynced ref. Persisted store is source of truth (store->setTheme); next-themes changes mirror back to the store only when they did NOT originate from our own sync (ref guard breaks the echo). Verified live via chrome-devtools: appTheme='dark' + theme='system' loads with html data-theme=dark, store/next-themes both converge to dark, zero console errors, no loop. SettingsForm (sets both store+theme) and the (demo)/demo page (sets theme only) both still work.

bugs.error_message: Playing vs Stockfish, idle a few mins, return shows game already lost but no checkmate
bugs.root_cause: Computer games were created with a finite server clock (timeControlSeconds from the setup time-control picker). Engine applyMove -> isTimeout flags on real wall-clock elapsed since last move (nowMs - lastTickAt), so idling between moves drained the human clock and the next submit returned status=completed reason=timeout. But the in-game UI hardcodes clock "∞" / Time "Unlimited" and shows no countdown, so the timeout loss was an unexplained surprise (no checkmate).
bugs.fix: Made vs-computer games untimed to match the UI. submitMove now resets engineState.clock.lastTickAt = nowMs before applyMove, so isTimeout sees ~0 elapsed and never fires regardless of idle time. Removed the time-control picker from computer-game-setup.tsx; it now sends fixed UNTIMED_SECONDS=600/inc=0 only to satisfy the DTO/category column. Kept the existing flag-fall guard as a defensive dead path (bug-005 mock test still passes). typecheck clean (api+web), 16/16 service spec green.

bugs.error_message: session 09 (a11y-polish) FAILED in 10m44s (exit 97): declared deliverables missing from session diff: apps/web/test/computer-game/use-game-keyboard.test.ts (not in session diff)
bugs.root_cause: Two layers. (1) Plan declared the keyboard-hook test at apps/web/test/computer-game/use-game-keyboard.test.ts but the hook lives at src/hooks/, so run 1's agent wrote the test to test/hooks/. (2) Deeper cause: the sonnet session is NON-DETERMINISTIC about test file paths/names across reruns. Run 1 produced test/hooks/use-game-keyboard.test.ts + test/computer-game/live-announcer.test.tsx; run 2 (after fixing the declared path) produced a single test/computer-game/a11y.test.tsx instead. The epic deliverables gate matches declared produces paths exactly, so chasing exact filenames was whack-a-mole. The two src deliverables (hook + live-announcer.tsx) were stable across runs; only the test path drifted.
bugs.fix: Changed the test deliverable in session-09 plan touches+produces from an exact path to a glob: apps/web/test/*.test.ts* . The validator compiles globs to regex where * becomes .* and crosses / (validate-session-deliverables.py _matches_declared), so it matches any test under apps/web/test/ regardless of subdir/name. Kept the two stable src files exact. Each resume also requires pruning the stale epic/vs-computer--s09 worktree+branch (always ahead of trunk after a failed run, so the runner refuses to delete it and aborts at exit 1). Verified run 2's actual diff (test/computer-game/a11y.test.tsx) matches the glob.

bugs.error_message: Both fly.dev apps unreachable on 80/443 (TLS handshake timeout) while machines were healthy; port 4000/3000 exposed raw to the internet
bugs.root_cause: CI ran `flyctl deploy --app X --dockerfile ...` WITHOUT -c fly.toml, so the repo config (http_service with 80/443 handlers, release_command, env, checks) was never applied — machines kept a bare raw-TCP service on the internal port. Separately, flyctl resolves the [build].dockerfile path relative to the CONFIG dir, so 'apps/api/Dockerfile' inside apps/api/fly.toml doubled to apps/api/apps/api/Dockerfile.
bugs.fix: deploy.yml now runs `flyctl deploy . -c apps/<x>/fly.toml --remote-only`; fly.tomls use dockerfile = "Dockerfile" (config-dir relative) with `.` as build context.

bugs.error_message: Prod register failed 'Failed to fetch'; even with CORS fixed the session would not persist cross-site
bugs.root_cause: CORS allowlist only had purechess.com (not purchased/pointed yet) so purechess-web.fly.dev got no Access-Control-Allow-Origin; and the session cookie was SameSite=Lax — *.fly.dev is on the Public Suffix List so web↔api are different registrable sites and Lax cookies are never sent on cross-site XHR.
bugs.fix: Added https://purechess-web.fly.dev to CORS origins; cookie is SameSite=None+Secure in production (clearCookie matches); WEB_URL/NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL/SITE_URL point at purechess-web.fly.dev until the domain is bought.

bugs.error_message: Socket.IO game:join silently dropped: client joined room but never received game:state pushes; board stuck at stale ply while polling heartbeat (10s) was the only updater
bugs.root_cause: Session auth ran in handleConnection (async sessions.lookupSession). Nest registers message handlers immediately, so the client's first game:join raced the pending lookup, read socket.data.userId === undefined and returned without joining the room. The socket still reported connected, so the web client relaxed polling to the slow heartbeat — worst of both worlds.
bugs.fix: Moved auth into a socket.io handshake middleware (server.use in afterInit): middleware completes before the client 'connect' event fires, so userId is always set before any game:join. Unauthorized handshakes get next(new Error('unauthorized')) -> connect_error client-side, polling stays fast.

bugs.error_message: hero-board replay test: board stuck mid-replay under fake timers (overlay frozen at transition:none) + piece-count assertions wrong (expected 24/26, actual 23/24)
bugs.root_cause: A single act(() => vi.advanceTimersByTime(5000)) runs all timers before React flushes effects, so an effect-chained setTimeout/rAF timeline (each step's timer is scheduled by the effect that runs AFTER the previous timer's setState commits) never advances past the first step. Also miscounted FEN pieces by hand (r1bk3r = 4 pieces, not 5).
bugs.fix: Advance fake time in 100ms slices, each in its own act() (runReplayToEnd helper), so effects flush between timer fires and schedule the next step; corrected piece counts by counting FEN letters programmatically (final=23, pre-sac=24).

bugs.error_message: Lost-update race in RatingsService: two rated games sharing a player finishing near-simultaneously could overwrite each other's rating effect (both computed from the same stale base; absolute writes)
bugs.root_cause: Rating rows were read and Glicko-2 computed BEFORE the transaction; the per-game whiteRatingAfter-IS-NULL gate serializes one game's two finalize paths but not two different games sharing a Rating row. persistRating writes absolute values, so the second commit erased the first.
bugs.fix: Moved the read+compute inside the transaction behind SELECT ... ORDER BY id FOR UPDATE row locks on both Rating rows (ordered to avoid deadlock); the gate claims first (whiteRatingAfter: 0 marker), snapshots are written from the locked re-read. Spec asserts compute-from-locked-read via a stale-pre-tx/fresh-in-tx fixture.

bugs.error_message: Live PvP client could show a rolled-back/stale board: WS pushes dropped during the submitting window were never recovered, and poll/resync REST responses overwrote newer WS-merged state (incl. regressing completed -> active)
bugs.root_cause: onState returned early while submitting with no follow-up; poll and onResync merges had no staleness guard, so an older in-flight REST snapshot could land after a newer WS push (10s slow-poll meant the regression persisted). A 400 on submitMove rolled back to a stale 'active' snapshot with no refetch.
bugs.fix: isStaleState(cur,next) guard (ply regression with same status, or completed->active) applied to EVERY merge path (WS, poll, resync, flag-claim); pendingResyncRef schedules a guarded refetch when the submitting window closes; submitMove errors trigger a guarded refetch after rollback.

bugs.error_message: Hero replay's data-no-animations guard was dead code: the attribute is only emitted by a mounted Chessboard, and no Chessboard exists on marketing surfaces — users with animations off still got the replay. The unit test passed by fabricating the attribute on document.body.
bugs.root_cause: Guard checked document.querySelector('[data-no-animations]'), an attribute only chessboard.tsx renders; home/play/auth surfaces mount no Chessboard and no BoardSettingsProvider (so the zustand store is not even rehydrated there).
bugs.fix: Guard reads the persisted settings envelope from localStorage directly (purechess-settings -> state.animations === false); test now sets the real production signal (localStorage) instead of stamping the attribute.

bugs.error_message: Legacy NULL-inviteColorChoice rows previewed a definitive color ('You play Black') while accept still randomized them — the exact bug A4 fixed, re-encoded for legacy rows and locked in by tests
bugs.root_cause: getInviteByToken's legacy fallback used the concrete slot color, but acceptInvite's legacy heuristic treats white-slot-creator+empty-black as random.
bugs.fix: Preview fallback mirrors accept's heuristic: white-slot legacy rows -> colorChoice 'random', black-slot -> 'black'. Specs updated to assert the honest preview.

bugs.error_message: Epic wave 2 sessions 03-06 all exit 1; exec logs empty. Transcript synthetic msg: "You've hit your session limit - resets 12am (America/Costa_Rica)"
bugs.root_cause: Claude CLI usage/session limit hit mid-epic (5 parallel sonnet sessions burned quota). claude -p exits 1 silently; epic runner logs only stderr which is empty.
bugs.fix: Wait for limit reset, resume with run-sessions.sh --start 3 --branch epic/purechess-category-best --base main. Diagnose via ~/.claude/projects/<worktree-slug>/*.jsonl synthetic assistant messages.

bugs.error_message: worktree for session 3 is in use by another process ... refusing to delete; aborting
bugs.root_cause: Two causes: (1) orphan nest --watch dev servers from failed sessions still held worktree dirs; (2) macOS BSD fuser exits 0 with no PIDs even when dir unused, so is_worktree_in_use() always true once dir exists.
bugs.fix: Kill orphan processes (ps -axo pid,command | grep epic--<name>), then git worktree remove --force each per-session worktree + git worktree prune, then resume runner.

bugs.error_message: thread 'main' panicked at src/pipeline/collapse.rs:291:30: end byte index 57 is not a char boundary; it is inside '\u{80}'

bugs.fix: None needed for epic (succeeded). Trust result.json over wrapper exit code when omni wraps long-running commands. Upstream omni bug.

bugs.error_message: Dark-theme .shadow-elevated override (literal box-shadow at [data-theme=dark] specificity) silently killed hover:shadow-brass-glow, active:shadow-none and ring-* utilities on any element also carrying shadow-elevated — the diff removed the very affordances it was adding, in the default theme
bugs.root_cause: A literal box-shadow rule outside @layer with (0,2,0)/(0,3,0) specificity after @tailwind utilities bypasses the --tw-shadow/--tw-ring-shadow composition pipeline and beats every shadow/ring utility on the element
bugs.fix: Theme the VALUE not the rule: per-theme --shadow-elevated CSS var consumed by tailwind boxShadow.elevated = var(--shadow-elevated); deleted the two override rules so utilities compose again

bugs.error_message: Promotion picker declared aria-modal=true with no focus trap: Tab escaped into aria-inert page content, Escape died once focus left the overlay, and closing stranded focus on body
bugs.root_cause: aria-modal asserts modality but raw divs provide none of it; React onKeyDown only sees events from focused descendants
bugs.fix: Tab/Shift+Tab loop across the four piece buttons, document-level capture-phase Escape listener, pre-dialog activeElement captured via lazy ref init (before commit-time autoFocus) and restored on unmount with [role=grid] fallback

bugs.error_message: disabled + disabled:pointer-events-none on review seek buttons dropped keyboard focus to body whenever the focused button became disabled at the start/end ply
bugs.root_cause: Natively disabling a focused element ejects focus; pointer-events-none additionally hides it from pointer users

bugs.error_message: Production live games fell back to slow REST polling instead of WebSocket push between two browsers.
bugs.root_cause: Web build baked NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL='' (same-origin /api proxy) and left NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL unset, so use-game-socket.ts WS_URL fell through to the NODE_ENV==='production' fallback branch. WS_URL is now deterministic regardless of the NODE_ENV branch. Verified baked into play/[gameId] chunk; live two-client proof shows engine.io polling->websocket upgrade + game:state push over websocket in ~0.9s.

bugs.error_message: All authenticated e2e flows 401 — test sessions never validate through SessionAuthGuard
bugs.root_cause: TestingService hard-coded the session HMAC secret as the literal 'test-secret', but SessionsService.lookupSession HMACs with the configured SESSION_SECRET (validated >=32 chars). The two could never match, so every test-created session was rejected (401) by SessionAuthGuard — live game board, review, reconnect, resign all failed to load.
bugs.fix: TestingService injects ConfigService and derives the session id via createHmac('sha256', config.get('SESSION_SECRET')) — identical to SessionsService. Run the e2e API with a >=32-char SESSION_SECRET shared by both.

bugs.error_message: User: 'sometimes when I drag a piece it drags a bunch but just like the images not the actual pieces' (native multi-image drag ghost)
bugs.root_cause: Board grid has no select-none and left-button pointerdown never preventDefault()s (use-drag onPointerDown only records start pos; chessboard onPointerDown left branch only clearShapes()). A mouse drag that the browser interprets as text selection sweeps across squares; a subsequent drag on that selection triggers the native drag-selection ghost showing every piece in the range. Piece imgs have draggable={false}+select-none but the selection RANGE spanning them still renders them in the OS drag image. Safari additionally needs -webkit-user-drag:none (draggable attr alone unreliable).
bugs.fix: FIXED. (1) Added `select-none` to the board grid container (chessboard.tsx) — kills text-selection across squares so a selection-drag can't spawn the multi-image native ghost. (2) Added `[-webkit-user-drag:none]` to the piece className (piece.tsx) — Safari ignores draggable={false}, this stops the single-image native drag. Verified the Tailwind arbitrary prop compiles to `-webkit-user-drag:none`. Skipped the pointerdown preventDefault option — redundant with select-none and it would have suppressed grid focus (typed-move input needs tabIndex=0 focus). 187 board unit tests + tsc green.

bugs.error_message: Invalid move: {"from":"e3","to":"e5"} — puzzle solution[0] illegal from computed start position (wrong side to move)
bugs.root_cause: Misread Lichess puzzle `initialPly`. It is the 0-based index of the game's LAST move (the setup move); the puzzle position is the FEN AFTER that move. buildContext used plies[initialPly-1].fenAfter, landing the board one ply early with the opponent (not the solver) to move, so solution[0] was unplayable.
bugs.fix: puzzleFen = plies[initialPly].fenAfter; setupMove = plies[initialPly]; preSetupFen = plies[initialPly-1].fenAfter (or START_FEN when initialPly===0). Guard plies.length > initialPly. Verified against live /api/puzzles/daily: full 9-move solution legal from the corrected FEN.

bugs.error_message: PostgresError 42883: function max(boolean) does not exist — /api/train/plan returned 500, breaking the training hub
bugs.root_cause: EndgamesService.list()/getBySlug() aggregated user pass/fail with prisma `_max: { succeeded: true }` on the BOOLEAN column. Postgres has no max(boolean) aggregate, so the groupBy/aggregate threw against a real DB. The unit specs mocked Prisma, so the SQL error never surfaced — it only broke at runtime (caught via E2E /train/plan, which calls insights→endgames gaps).
bugs.fix: Replaced the boolean _max with COUNTs of succeeded attempts: list() does two groupBys (all attempts → attempted; where succeeded:true → solved iff count>0); getBySlug() does two count() calls. Updated endgames.service.spec.ts mocks to the new two-read shape. solved = (success count > 0).

bugs.error_message: ESLint: React Hook useCallback has a missing dependency: 'theme' (training-session.tsx:191) — stale-closure: analytics puzzleSolved/puzzleFailed tag the wrong theme if the theme prop changes without remount

bugs.error_message: Browser: 'Connecting to http://localhost:4100/api/auth/register violates CSP connect-src' + 'Failed to fetch' on register — client bundle posted to :4100 while served CSP/API allowed only :4000
bugs.root_cause: ENV/stale-cache, not a repo bug. A prior `next dev` run started with NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://localhost:4100 (alt-port) compiled client chunks with :4100 baked in (NEXT_PUBLIC_* is inlined at compile). The current dev process started with :4000, so the CSP header (computed from env at startup, next.config.mjs:15-18) said :4000, but next dev served the cached :4100 chunks for un-recompiled pages. Result: mixed 4000/4100 in .next/static -> CSP blocked the client fetch.
bugs.fix: Stop the web dev server, `rm -rf apps/web/.next`, restart `next dev` with NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://localhost:4000 (matching .env + the API on :4000). GOTCHA: changing NEXT_PUBLIC_* requires clearing .next; a half-restarted dev server serves stale inlined env in cached chunks.

bugs.fix: Changed to /([a-zA-Z])(\d+)(?![a-zA-Z])/g — number splits only when trailing. 'mateIn1'->'Mate in 1', 'e2etest'->'E2etest'. NOTE: puzzle-board.tsx:20 has a DUPLICATE humanizeTheme with the same bug (out of scope).

bugs.error_message: Duplicated code: humanizeTheme defined twice + lib/api fetch boilerplate (API base + apiFetch/ensureOk) copy-pasted across 8 client files
bugs.root_cause: Each lib/api/*.ts re-declared the API origin const and a near-identical fetch helper; puzzle-board.tsx had a weaker local humanizeTheme copy.
bugs.fix: puzzle-board.tsx now imports humanizeTheme from theme-tile. New lib/api/client.ts exports API_BASE + apiFetch + ensureOk; training/endgames/repertoire/matchmaking/pvp-games/auth/computer-games/reports import it. jscpd: 51->45 clones, 1050->931 dup lines. typecheck/lint clean, web 662 tests green.

bugs.root_cause: onResign: _isGameOver ? undefined : handleResign, → _isGameOver ? undefined : _canAbort ? handleAbort : handleResign,
bugs.fix: Changed onResign: _isGameOver ? undefined : handleResign, → _isGameOver ? undefined : _canAbort ? handleAbort : handleResign,

bugs.root_cause: No early return/throw for edge case: rematchMinePending || submitting

bugs.error_message: PvP games ending by resign/timeout/draw-agreement exported a PGN with a stale '*' result tag
bugs.root_cause: GamesService.finalize() persisted status/result but never rebuilt the PGN — only submitMove rebuilt it, so any non-move completion path left the last move's '*' tag
bugs.fix: finalize() now rebuilds the PGN with the real result tag (1-0/0-1/1/2-1/2) via engine.buildPgn and persists it in the same guarded updateMany; emitted WS payload carries the new pgn

bugs.root_cause: Async call without await — returned Promise instead of value
Had "mt-12 grid gap-5 sm:grid-cols-2"
bugs.fix: Changed to "mt-12 grid gap-5 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3"

bugs.root_cause: name: 'Casual' → /^Casual/

bugs.error_message: Wrong reference: Friend should be Live
bugs.root_cause: Used "Friend" instead of "Live"

bugs.error_message: TypeError: request is not a function (every API e2e suite)
bugs.root_cause: import * as request from 'supertest' with esModuleInterop:true produces a non-callable namespace object — the e2e suite could never have passed locally
bugs.fix: changed to default import `import request from 'supertest'` across test/e2e/

bugs.error_message: 500 Internal Server Error on every authed e2e route: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'purechess_session')
bugs.root_cause: createApp (TestingModule) never registered cookie-parser, which main.ts does — guards read req.cookies and crashed
bugs.fix: app.use(cookieParser()) in createApp, mirroring main.ts; also fixed stale endpoint expectations (invites `code`->inviteToken, admin PATCH->POST disable + audit-log->audit, games /:id -> /:id/state). Full e2e suite green (21/21) for the first time

bugs.error_message: Matchmaking queue retained a ghost ZSET entry for a player already paired into a game
bugs.root_cause: status() read the queue-entry hash, then an opponent's join claimed+deleted it, then status()'s mmJoin re-scan re-enqueued the stale snapshot — a third joiner could claim a player already in a game
bugs.fix: mmJoin Lua takes a mustExist flag ('1' on status re-scans): if mm:u:{self} is gone, return '__claimed__' and do NOT re-enqueue; the pairing arrives via the mm:match mailbox on the next poll

bugs.error_message: Sculpted queen 'crown panel' specular ellipse straddled the open V-notch between crown spikes — painted a ~10-13% white glaze on bare board pixels between the spikes (both wQ/bQ) and fed the CSS drop-shadow
bugs.root_cause: Overlay ellipses paint on the raw SVG canvas, not clipped to the piece silhouette; the panel center (18.5,20.5) sat in the gap between crown spikes
bugs.fix: Re-seated the highlight onto the central spike face E(22.3,19,1.5,3.4,rot -8), verified extents against the spike flank lines, regenerated the set

bugs.error_message: Legacy persisted pieceSet 'standard' merged into the store unmodified forever — field typed PieceSetId held an out-of-union value; future `s.pieceSet === 'sculpted'` comparisons would silently misbehave for legacy users
bugs.root_cause: zustand persist shallow-merges the old envelope with no version/migrate/merge; only read-site fallbacks (pieceSetBase) masked it
bugs.fix: Added a persist `merge` that coerces unrecognized pieceSet ids to DEFAULT_PIECE_SET at the boundary; pieceSetBase fallback kept as defense-in-depth

bugs.error_message: bQ orb glints invisible at game size (sized for wQ's r=2.0 orbs, not bQ's r=2.75) and bN speculars sat low-right against the key light / too faint to register — bN and bQ orbs were the flattest black elements
bugs.root_cause: Glint scaled to the wrong orb radius; knight neck sheen placed on the mane side instead of the lit chest ridge; crest alpha 0.13 contributed nothing
bugs.fix: Glints up to E(cx-0.9,cy-1.0,1.4,1.2,b:0.62) (max extent 2.745 <= orb r 2.75); knight overlays moved to mane-top ridge E(26.5,11.4,b:0.22) + chest ridge E(26,20.5,b:0.2,rot 16); regenerated

bugs.error_message: Deploy CI 'Verify Production' failed: curl -sf api/health hung ~53s at the Fly edge then 5xx'd, 'API health failed post-deploy' — while the app itself was healthy seconds later
bugs.root_cause: Single un-retried curl raced the post-rolling-deploy window: with auto_stop_machines=true + 30s check grace_period, the Fly proxy can briefly have no passing machine to route to right after machine replacement; curl -sf treats the resulting held-then-5xx response as hard failure
bugs.fix: Verify steps now retry up to 10x with 10s backoff and --max-time 15, echoing the response body per attempt so a real failure is diagnosable; also bumped actions/checkout v4->v5 in deploy.yml+ci.yml (Node 20 actions force-switched to Node 24 on 2026-06-16)

bugs.error_message: rush-client.test.tsx: solving move never registered — rush-score stayed '0' after a correct click
bugs.root_cause: Synthetic test puzzles used a placeholder fen ('a2a3-fen'). useLocalPuzzle's onMove calls applyUci(fen, uci); on an invalid FEN applyUci returns null and the hook silently returns (if (!applied) return;) WITHOUT firing onSolved/onFailed, so the score/combo never moved.
bugs.fix: Gave every synthetic puzzle the standard start FEN + a legal pawn-push as moves[0] (a2a3..f2f3); the wrong move is another legal push (h2h4) that uciMatch (pure string compare) rejects.

bugs.error_message: S09 drill spec: expect(paths).toEqual(['0.0','0.0.0.0','0.1']) failed — got ['0.0.0.0','0.1']; dueLineCount expected 2 got 1
bugs.root_cause: Test fixture treated an INTERIOR node (e5 at path 0.0) as a drillable line. enumerateLines only emits LEAF paths (children.length===0); e5 continues into Nf3 so it is not a leaf. A due card on a non-leaf path is also ignored by getDrillLines (path-resolves check).
bugs.fix: Corrected the fixture: the 1.e4 e5 2.Nf3 Nc6 tree has exactly two leaf lines (0.0.0.0 and 0.1), not three. Re-pointed the due-card test to a real leaf path. All 12 spec tests pass.

bugs.error_message: Training drills (theme/rush/review) never announced solve outcome to screen readers; the result overlay was aria-hidden icons + non-live text.
bugs.root_cause:
The Chessboard's role=status region only narrates the MOVE (FEN diff), not the verdict. The visual overlays ('Solved'/'Incorrect') used aria-hidden lucide icons + plain

outside any live region, so SR users got no Correct/Not-the-move feedback or progress.
bugs.fix: Added a shared <TrainingAnnouncer> (one polite role=status aria-live region per surface, mirrors computer-game LiveAnnouncer). Each surface sets a verdict+progress string on settle ('Correct. Solved 3 of 10.' / 'Not the move — try again.' / rush strikes / 'Run over. Final score N.'). The board keeps narrating the move so the two never double-announce.

bugs.error_message: Auto-advance dropped keyboard focus: when a puzzle auto-advanced, a keyboard solver's focus stayed on a stale board / fell to <body> with no signal the next puzzle loaded.
bugs.root_cause: The classic auto-advance a11y break — the board re-renders with a new position but nothing restores focus to the grid for keyboard-only solvers.
bugs.fix: Added focusBoard(container): on each auto-advance (loadNext / index advance) a requestAnimationFrame re-focuses the board's role=grid — but ONLY when focus was already inside the board container (never steals focus from a mouse user reading elsewhere).

bugs.error_message: Solid-brass primary buttons crept into 3 training surfaces during Wave-3 parallel work — forbidden by design.md (brass solid is reserved for the auth door).
bugs.root_cause: Parallel S12/S13 sessions used bg-brass text-brass-foreground for the plan-row CTA, the featured insight 'Drill it' button, and the stats 'Practice X' button. design.md: in-app primary is bone (bg-foreground text-background); brass solid is only the sign-in/create-account door.
bugs.fix: Converted all three primary buttons to bone (bg-foreground text-background). daily-plan's completed-row 'Again' became a quiet outline. Brass STATUS accents kept (review-due badge, streak calendar cells) — those are within budget.

bugs.error_message: RushHud countdown pulse and goal-ring/progress fills honored prefers-reduced-motion but not the in-app 'animations off' settings switch; charts had only a generic aria-label.
bugs.root_cause: The HUD/goal-ring live outside the board's data-no-animations container, so the settings toggle never reached them. The rating chart's role=img aria-label was just 'Puzzle rating over time' (no data).
bugs.fix: RushHud now reads useBoardSettings() and drops the clock-pulse class when animationMs===0. Added motion-reduce:transition-none to the goal-ring fill, session progress bar, rush flash, and HUD transitions. Chart aria-label is now a text-equivalent summary (span/endpoints/net direction); streak calendar aria-label states active-day count + current streak.

bugs.error_message: chrome-devtools MCP wedged: 'The browser is already running for .../chrome-profile' on every call.
bugs.root_cause: An orphan MCP-spawned Chrome (--enable-automation, about:blank, pointed at the MCP user-data-dir) from a prior session held the profile SingletonLock; a competing MCP server instance owned it, so this session's server could not attach.
bugs.fix: Identified the orphan by its --enable-automation + chrome-devtools-mcp user-data-dir flags (NOT the user's interactive Chrome), killed that PID, removed the stale Singleton{Lock,Cookie,Socket} symlinks, then new_page succeeded. Captured all 8 screenshots after.

bugs.root_cause: Synthetic test puzzles used a placeholder fen ('a2a3-fen'). useLocalPuzzle's onMove calls applyUci(fen, uci); on an invalid FEN applyUci returns null and the hook silently returns (if (!applied) return;) WITHOUT firing onSolved/onFailed, so the score/combo never moved.
bugs.fix: Gave every synthetic puzzle the standard start FEN + a legal pawn-push as moves[0] (a2a3..f2f3); the wrong move is another legal push (h2h4) that uciMatch (pure string compare) rejects.

bugs.error_message: S09 drill spec: expect(paths).toEqual(['0.0','0.0.0.0','0.1']) failed — got ['0.0.0.0','0.1']; dueLineCount expected 2 got 1
bugs.root_cause: Test fixture treated an INTERIOR node (e5 at path 0.0) as a drillable line. enumerateLines only emits LEAF paths (children.length===0); e5 continues into Nf3 so it is not a leaf. A due card on a non-leaf path is also ignored by getDrillLines (path-resolves check).
bugs.fix: Corrected the fixture: the 1.e4 e5 2.Nf3 Nc6 tree has exactly two leaf lines (0.0.0.0 and 0.1), not three. Re-pointed the due-card test to a real leaf path. All 12 spec tests pass.

bugs.error_message: Training drills (theme/rush/review) never announced solve outcome to screen readers; the result overlay was aria-hidden icons + non-live text.
bugs.root_cause:
The Chessboard's role=status region only narrates the MOVE (FEN diff), not the verdict. The visual overlays ('Solved'/'Incorrect') used aria-hidden lucide icons + plain

outside any live region, so SR users got no Correct/Not-the-move feedback or progress.
bugs.fix: Added a shared <TrainingAnnouncer> (one polite role=status aria-live region per surface, mirrors computer-game LiveAnnouncer). Each surface sets a verdict+progress string on settle ('Correct. Solved 3 of 10.' / 'Not the move — try again.' / rush strikes / 'Run over. Final score N.'). The board keeps narrating the move so the two never double-announce.

bugs.error_message: Auto-advance dropped keyboard focus: when a puzzle auto-advanced, a keyboard solver's focus stayed on a stale board / fell to <body> with no signal the next puzzle loaded.
bugs.root_cause: The classic auto-advance a11y break — the board re-renders with a new position but nothing restores focus to the grid for keyboard-only solvers.
bugs.fix: Added focusBoard(container): on each auto-advance (loadNext / index advance) a requestAnimationFrame re-focuses the board's role=grid — but ONLY when focus was already inside the board container (never steals focus from a mouse user reading elsewhere).

bugs.error_message: Solid-brass primary buttons crept into 3 training surfaces during Wave-3 parallel work — forbidden by design.md (brass solid is reserved for the auth door).
bugs.root_cause: Parallel S12/S13 sessions used bg-brass text-brass-foreground for the plan-row CTA, the featured insight 'Drill it' button, and the stats 'Practice X' button. design.md: in-app primary is bone (bg-foreground text-background); brass solid is only the sign-in/create-account door.
bugs.fix: Converted all three primary buttons to bone (bg-foreground text-background). daily-plan's completed-row 'Again' became a quiet outline. Brass STATUS accents kept (review-due badge, streak calendar cells) — those are within budget.

bugs.error_message: RushHud countdown pulse and goal-ring/progress fills honored prefers-reduced-motion but not the in-app 'animations off' settings switch; charts had only a generic aria-label.
bugs.root_cause: The HUD/goal-ring live outside the board's data-no-animations container, so the settings toggle never reached them. The rating chart's role=img aria-label was just 'Puzzle rating over time' (no data).
bugs.fix: RushHud now reads useBoardSettings() and drops the clock-pulse class when animationMs===0. Added motion-reduce:transition-none to the goal-ring fill, session progress bar, rush flash, and HUD transitions. Chart aria-label is now a text-equivalent summary (span/endpoints/net direction); streak calendar aria-label states active-day count + current streak.

bugs.error_message: chrome-devtools MCP wedged: 'The browser is already running for .../chrome-profile' on every call.
bugs.root_cause: An orphan MCP-spawned Chrome (--enable-automation, about:blank, pointed at the MCP user-data-dir) from a prior session held the profile SingletonLock; a competing MCP server instance owned it, so this session's server could not attach.
bugs.fix: Identified the orphan by its --enable-automation + chrome-devtools-mcp user-data-dir flags (NOT the user's interactive Chrome), killed that PID, removed the stale Singleton{Lock,Cookie,Socket} symlinks, then new_page succeeded. Captured all 8 screenshots after.

bugs.root_cause: No early return/throw for edge case: rematchMinePending || submitting

bugs.error_message: PvP games ending by resign/timeout/draw-agreement exported a PGN with a stale '*' result tag
bugs.root_cause: GamesService.finalize() persisted status/result but never rebuilt the PGN — only submitMove rebuilt it, so any non-move completion path left the last move's '*' tag
bugs.fix: finalize() now rebuilds the PGN with the real result tag (1-0/0-1/1/2-1/2) via engine.buildPgn and persists it in the same guarded updateMany; emitted WS payload carries the new pgn

bugs.root_cause: Async call without await — returned Promise instead of value

bugs.fix: Changed to "mt-12 grid gap-5 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3"

bugs.root_cause: name: 'Casual' → /^Casual/

bugs.error_message: Wrong reference: Friend should be Live
bugs.root_cause: Used "Friend" instead of "Live"

bugs.error_message: TypeError: request is not a function (every API e2e suite)
bugs.root_cause: import * as request from 'supertest' with esModuleInterop:true produces a non-callable namespace object — the e2e suite could never have passed locally
bugs.fix: changed to default import `import request from 'supertest'` across test/e2e/

bugs.error_message: 500 Internal Server Error on every authed e2e route: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'purechess_session')
bugs.root_cause: createApp (TestingModule) never registered cookie-parser, which main.ts does — guards read req.cookies and crashed
bugs.fix: app.use(cookieParser()) in createApp, mirroring main.ts; also fixed stale endpoint expectations (invites `code`->inviteToken, admin PATCH->POST disable + audit-log->audit, games /:id -> /:id/state). Full e2e suite green (21/21) for the first time

bugs.error_message: Matchmaking queue retained a ghost ZSET entry for a player already paired into a game
bugs.root_cause: status() read the queue-entry hash, then an opponent's join claimed+deleted it, then status()'s mmJoin re-scan re-enqueued the stale snapshot — a third joiner could claim a player already in a game
bugs.fix: mmJoin Lua takes a mustExist flag ('1' on status re-scans): if mm:u:{self} is gone, return '__claimed__' and do NOT re-enqueue; the pairing arrives via the mm:match mailbox on the next poll

bugs.error_message: Sculpted queen 'crown panel' specular ellipse straddled the open V-notch between crown spikes — painted a ~10-13% white glaze on bare board pixels between the spikes (both wQ/bQ) and fed the CSS drop-shadow
bugs.root_cause: Overlay ellipses paint on the raw SVG canvas, not clipped to the piece silhouette; the panel center (18.5,20.5) sat in the gap between crown spikes
bugs.fix: Re-seated the highlight onto the central spike face E(22.3,19,1.5,3.4,rot -8), verified extents against the spike flank lines, regenerated the set

bugs.error_message: Legacy persisted pieceSet 'standard' merged into the store unmodified forever — field typed PieceSetId held an out-of-union value; future `s.pieceSet === 'sculpted'` comparisons would silently misbehave for legacy users
bugs.root_cause: zustand persist shallow-merges the old envelope with no version/migrate/merge; only read-site fallbacks (pieceSetBase) masked it
bugs.fix: Added a persist `merge` that coerces unrecognized pieceSet ids to DEFAULT_PIECE_SET at the boundary; pieceSetBase fallback kept as defense-in-depth

bugs.error_message: bQ orb glints invisible at game size (sized for wQ's r=2.0 orbs, not bQ's r=2.75) and bN speculars sat low-right against the key light / too faint to register — bN and bQ orbs were the flattest black elements
bugs.root_cause: Glint scaled to the wrong orb radius; knight neck sheen placed on the mane side instead of the lit chest ridge; crest alpha 0.13 contributed nothing
bugs.fix: Glints up to E(cx-0.9,cy-1.0,1.4,1.2,b:0.62) (max extent 2.745 <= orb r 2.75); knight overlays moved to mane-top ridge E(26.5,11.4,b:0.22) + chest ridge E(26,20.5,b:0.2,rot 16); regenerated

bugs.error_message: Deploy CI 'Verify Production' failed: curl -sf api/health hung ~53s at the Fly edge then 5xx'd, 'API health failed post-deploy' — while the app itself was healthy seconds later
bugs.root_cause: Single un-retried curl raced the post-rolling-deploy window: with auto_stop_machines=true + 30s check grace_period, the Fly proxy can briefly have no passing machine to route to right after machine replacement; curl -sf treats the resulting held-then-5xx response as hard failure

bugs.error_message: 500 Internal Server Error on every authed e2e route: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'purechess_session')
bugs.root_cause: createApp (TestingModule) never registered cookie-parser, which main.ts does — guards read req.cookies and crashed
bugs.fix: app.use(cookieParser()) in createApp, mirroring main.ts; also fixed stale endpoint expectations (invites `code`->inviteToken, admin PATCH->POST disable + audit-log->audit, games /:id -> /:id/state). Full e2e suite green (21/21) for the first time

bugs.error_message: Matchmaking queue retained a ghost ZSET entry for a player already paired into a game
bugs.root_cause: status() read the queue-entry hash, then an opponent's join claimed+deleted it, then status()'s mmJoin re-scan re-enqueued the stale snapshot — a third joiner could claim a player already in a game
bugs.fix: mmJoin Lua takes a mustExist flag ('1' on status re-scans): if mm:u:{self} is gone, return '__claimed__' and do NOT re-enqueue; the pairing arrives via the mm:match mailbox on the next poll

bugs.error_message: Sculpted queen 'crown panel' specular ellipse straddled the open V-notch between crown spikes — painted a ~10-13% white glaze on bare board pixels between the spikes (both wQ/bQ) and fed the CSS drop-shadow
bugs.root_cause: Overlay ellipses paint on the raw SVG canvas, not clipped to the piece silhouette; the panel center (18.5,20.5) sat in the gap between crown spikes
bugs.fix: Re-seated the highlight onto the central spike face E(22.3,19,1.5,3.4,rot -8), verified extents against the spike flank lines, regenerated the set

bugs.error_message: Legacy persisted pieceSet 'standard' merged into the store unmodified forever — field typed PieceSetId held an out-of-union value; future `s.pieceSet === 'sculpted'` comparisons would silently misbehave for legacy users
bugs.root_cause: zustand persist shallow-merges the old envelope with no version/migrate/merge; only read-site fallbacks (pieceSetBase) masked it
bugs.fix: Added a persist `merge` that coerces unrecognized pieceSet ids to DEFAULT_PIECE_SET at the boundary; pieceSetBase fallback kept as defense-in-depth

bugs.error_message: bQ orb glints invisible at game size (sized for wQ's r=2.0 orbs, not bQ's r=2.75) and bN speculars sat low-right against the key light / too faint to register — bN and bQ orbs were the flattest black elements
bugs.root_cause: Glint scaled to the wrong orb radius; knight neck sheen placed on the mane side instead of the lit chest ridge; crest alpha 0.13 contributed nothing
bugs.fix: Glints up to E(cx-0.9,cy-1.0,1.4,1.2,b:0.62) (max extent 2.745 <= orb r 2.75); knight overlays moved to mane-top ridge E(26.5,11.4,b:0.22) + chest ridge E(26,20.5,b:0.2,rot 16); regenerated

bugs.error_message: Deploy CI 'Verify Production' failed: curl -sf api/health hung ~53s at the Fly edge then 5xx'd, 'API health failed post-deploy' — while the app itself was healthy seconds later
bugs.root_cause: Single un-retried curl raced the post-rolling-deploy window: with auto_stop_machines=true + 30s check grace_period, the Fly proxy can briefly have no passing machine to route to right after machine replacement; curl -sf treats the resulting held-then-5xx response as hard failure
bugs.fix: Verify steps now retry up to 10x with 10s backoff and --max-time 15, echoing the response body per attempt so a real failure is diagnosable; also bumped actions/checkout v4->v5 in deploy.yml+ci.yml (Node 20 actions force-switched to Node 24 on 2026-06-16)

bugs.error_message: rush-client.test.tsx: solving move never registered — rush-score stayed '0' after a correct click
bugs.root_cause: Synthetic test puzzles used a placeholder fen ('a2a3-fen'). useLocalPuzzle's onMove calls applyUci(fen, uci); on an invalid FEN applyUci returns null and the hook silently returns (if (!applied) return;) WITHOUT firing onSolved/onFailed, so the score/combo never moved.
bugs.fix: Gave every synthetic puzzle the standard start FEN + a legal pawn-push as moves[0] (a2a3..f2f3); the wrong move is another legal push (h2h4) that uciMatch (pure string compare) rejects.

bugs.error_message: S09 drill spec: expect(paths).toEqual(['0.0','0.0.0.0','0.1']) failed — got ['0.0.0.0','0.1']; dueLineCount expected 2 got 1
bugs.root_cause: Test fixture treated an INTERIOR node (e5 at path 0.0) as a drillable line. enumerateLines only emits LEAF paths (children.length===0); e5 continues into Nf3 so it is not a leaf. A due card on a non-leaf path is also ignored by getDrillLines (path-resolves check).
bugs.fix: Corrected the fixture: the 1.e4 e5 2.Nf3 Nc6 tree has exactly two leaf lines (0.0.0.0 and 0.1), not three. Re-pointed the due-card test to a real leaf path. All 12 spec tests pass.

bugs.error_message: Training drills (theme/rush/review) never announced solve outcome to screen readers; the result overlay was aria-hidden icons + non-live text.
bugs.root_cause:
The Chessboard's role=status region only narrates the MOVE (FEN diff), not the verdict. The visual overlays ('Solved'/'Incorrect') used aria-hidden lucide icons + plain

outside any live region, so SR users got no Correct/Not-the-move feedback or progress.
bugs.fix: Added a shared <TrainingAnnouncer> (one polite role=status aria-live region per surface, mirrors computer-game LiveAnnouncer). Each surface sets a verdict+progress string on settle ('Correct. Solved 3 of 10.' / 'Not the move — try again.' / rush strikes / 'Run over. Final score N.'). The board keeps narrating the move so the two never double-announce.

bugs.error_message: Auto-advance dropped keyboard focus: when a puzzle auto-advanced, a keyboard solver's focus stayed on a stale board / fell to <body> with no signal the next puzzle loaded.
bugs.root_cause: The classic auto-advance a11y break — the board re-renders with a new position but nothing restores focus to the grid for keyboard-only solvers.
bugs.fix: Added focusBoard(container): on each auto-advance (loadNext / index advance) a requestAnimationFrame re-focuses the board's role=grid — but ONLY when focus was already inside the board container (never steals focus from a mouse user reading elsewhere).

bugs.error_message: Solid-brass primary buttons crept into 3 training surfaces during Wave-3 parallel work — forbidden by design.md (brass solid is reserved for the auth door).
bugs.root_cause: Parallel S12/S13 sessions used bg-brass text-brass-foreground for the plan-row CTA, the featured insight 'Drill it' button, and the stats 'Practice X' button. design.md: in-app primary is bone (bg-foreground text-background); brass solid is only the sign-in/create-account door.
bugs.fix: Converted all three primary buttons to bone (bg-foreground text-background). daily-plan's completed-row 'Again' became a quiet outline. Brass STATUS accents kept (review-due badge, streak calendar cells) — those are within budget.

bugs.error_message: RushHud countdown pulse and goal-ring/progress fills honored prefers-reduced-motion but not the in-app 'animations off' settings switch; charts had only a generic aria-label.
bugs.root_cause: The HUD/goal-ring live outside the board's data-no-animations container, so the settings toggle never reached them. The rating chart's role=img aria-label was just 'Puzzle rating over time' (no data).
bugs.fix: RushHud now reads useBoardSettings() and drops the clock-pulse class when animationMs===0. Added motion-reduce:transition-none to the goal-ring fill, session progress bar, rush flash, and HUD transitions. Chart aria-label is now a text-equivalent summary (span/endpoints/net direction); streak calendar aria-label states active-day count + current streak.

bugs.error_message: chrome-devtools MCP wedged: 'The browser is already running for .../chrome-profile' on every call.
bugs.root_cause: An orphan MCP-spawned Chrome (--enable-automation, about:blank, pointed at the MCP user-data-dir) from a prior session held the profile SingletonLock; a competing MCP server instance owned it, so this session's server could not attach.
bugs.fix: Identified the orphan by its --enable-automation + chrome-devtools-mcp user-data-dir flags (NOT the user's interactive Chrome), killed that PID, removed the stale Singleton{Lock,Cookie,Socket} symlinks, then new_page succeeded. Captured all 8 screenshots after.

Used "!==" instead of "||"
Property access on potentially null/undefined value
bugs.fix: Added null safety (optional chaining or null check) | Also: const _isGameOver = _isPlaying && isTerminal(state.game.stat; // The socket (and slow heartbeat) stay up on finished games | Also: // Draw-offer / rematch transitions, mirrored to one polite ; // SR users hear offers arrive/resolve without the visuals. | Also: /**; * Shared template for non-move POST actions: optimistic-free | Also: const isAborted = game.status === 'aborted';; const isGameOver = isTerminal(game.status);

bugs.root_cause: onResign: _isGameOver ? undefined : handleResign, → _isGameOver ? undefined : _canAbort ? handleAbort : handleResign,
bugs.fix: Changed onResign: _isGameOver ? undefined : handleResign, → _isGameOver ? undefined : _canAbort ? handleAbort : handleResign,

bugs.root_cause: No early return/throw for edge case: rematchMinePending || submitting

bugs.error_message: PvP games ending by resign/timeout/draw-agreement exported a PGN with a stale '*' result tag
bugs.root_cause: GamesService.finalize() persisted status/result but never rebuilt the PGN — only submitMove rebuilt it, so any non-move completion path left the last move's '*' tag
bugs.fix: finalize() now rebuilds the PGN with the real result tag (1-0/0-1/1/2-1/2) via engine.buildPgn and persists it in the same guarded updateMany; emitted WS payload carries the new pgn

bugs.error_message: invariant expected app router to be mounted (useRouter in PracticeFromFenDialog) — review-page.test.tsx + analyze tests failed (17 failures)
bugs.root_cause: PracticeFromFenDialog calls useRouter() unconditionally and was always mounted in the board components. Existing board tests don't mock next/navigation, so the hook threw outside an app-router context even though the dialog was closed.
bugs.fix: Conditionally mount the dialog only when practiceOpen is true ({practiceOpen && <PracticeFromFenDialog open ... />}). useRouter then runs only after the user opens it; closed boards never invoke it, so untouched tests stay green.

bugs.error_message: All computer difficulty levels feel the same
bugs.root_cause: driveBot only passed computerLevel to getBestMove; eloTarget/styleBlunderCp/thinkTimeMs from setup were sent to POST /computer-games but never persisted or applied. LEVEL_PROFILES also clustered levels 1-3, 4-5, 6-8 into identical play.
bugs.fix: Persist computerEngine config in engineState JSON; return eloTarget/styleBlunderCp/thinkTimeMs in ComputerGameStateDto; driveBot uses getComputerMove(state) with resolveComputerMoveOptions; widened LEVEL_PROFILES ELO/skill spread.

bugs.error_message: CI nest build TS2345: withPersistedEngineConfig prior arg partial object
bugs.root_cause: createGame passed { computerEngine } stub to withPersistedEngineConfig which expects full SerializableEngineState
bugs.fix: Spread engineConfig onto toSerializable result directly in createGame

bugs.error_message: CI Unit Tests: training-session + training-surfaces TrainingSession tests flake — recordAttempt 0 calls, announcer empty
bugs.root_cause: Mock Chessboard ignored readOnly and tests clicked solve-btn as soon as it mounted, before fetchNextPuzzle settled and useLocalPuzzle entered player phase; noop onMove under CI load
bugs.fix: Mock respects readOnly/disabled; clickSolve waits for 'Find the best move' prompt + enabled button; vitest.setup afterEach also vi.clearAllTimers()

bugs.error_message: Opening drill board renders tiny after starting a session from /openings
bugs.root_cause: Board sizing used var(--top-bar) in calc() but --top-bar was never defined in globals.css, invalidating lg:h-[calc(...)] and height-driven lg:h-full board wrapper; flex-1 height chain on the drill grid was also missing
bugs.fix: Defined --top-bar: 3.5rem in globals.css :root; opening-drill grid uses flex-1 min-h-0; board max reserve bumped to 16rem; drill page wrappers get min-h-0

bugs.error_message: chess.com Sync recent games completes with 0 games and no error
bugs.root_cause: archivePath() built YYYY/M (unpadded month) but chess.com API requires YYYY/MM; every archive fetch 404'd and returned an empty games array silently
bugs.fix: Zero-pad month in archivePath(); log when zero games; client sync hook shows notice on empty fetch and uses runningRef guard

bugs.error_message: CI unit tests failed: analyze-client (invariant expected app router to be mounted) + eval-panel (text-[#10140f]/text-[#e9e4d4] assertions stale)
bugs.root_cause: AnalyzeClient now renders AppShell→UserMenu (useRouter/usePathname/useQueryClient) without test mocks; EvalBar score cap migrated to semantic tokens (text-background/text-board-light) but tests still asserted hardcoded hex classes
bugs.fix: analyze-client.test: mock next/navigation + auth getMe, wrap in QueryClientProvider; eval-panel.test: assert text-background and text-board-light

bugs.error_message: Openings hub 'Train the line' banner does nothing after chess.com sync
bugs.root_cause: Opening weakness actionHref points to /openings?chesscom= or /openings?repertoire= but OpeningsClient never read URL params; banner was a same-page Link so navigation produced no visible change
bugs.fix: Added opening-deep-link resolver; OpeningsClient handles ?chesscom/?repertoire (drill matching repertoire, else Opening Lab); banner is now a button calling the resolver directly

bugs.error_message: Train the line opens Opening Lab as a clean slate — no opening loaded
bugs.root_cause: chess.com/ECO labels use comma separators that do not match lichess colon names; deep link only passed ?q= with no mistake FEN; Opening Lab did not resolve labels robustly or sync URL params on client navigation
bugs.fix: findOpeningForLabel() normalizes labels + matches by FEN epd; actionHref now /openings/lab?q=&fen=; client enriches chesscom links with stored mistake FEN; Opening Lab applies deep link once per URL

bugs.error_message: chess.com sync banner showed C20 and sent users to Opening Lab with only e4 — no visible mistake analysis
bugs.root_cause: PGN ECO tags stored as raw codes (C20); chess.com weakness actionHref pointed at Opening Lab (repertoire browser) instead of the mistake list; Lab cannot show per-game engine analysis
bugs.fix: displayOpeningLabel resolves ECO codes; Train/Fix routes to highlighted chess-com-mistakes panel; Study position + Browse in Lab per row; clearer copy explaining Stockfish sync analysis
